behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: Seam map: module/interface seam between manifest validation and plugin execution pipeline (loader -> validator -> importer). The real failure is ordering across modules: if validation happens too late, plugin code can execute first. Unit tests of validator alone miss that cross-module gate. Rejection gate: System was considered, but behavior text only requires local loader sequencing and does not require subprocess, real network, or OS-specific primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with one plugin whose manifest.json omits a required field and whose entry module writes a sentinel side effect when imported. Run the real loader over that directory. Assert: (1) loader reports manifest validation failure, (2) sentinel side effect is absent (entry module never executed), and (3) onLoad was never called.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's onLoad hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: Seam map: module/interface seam across dependency graph resolution and lifecycle dispatcher. Failure appears when resolver output is handed to hook executor in wrong order; testing either module alone is insufficient. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement, no AI/ML output dependency, no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Set up two real test plugins in a temp directory: A depends on B. Each onLoad appends its name to a shared in-memory or file-backed log. Execute loader and assert log order is ["B","A"]. Also assert both plugins reached loaded state.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Seam map: module/interface seam between dependency parser, cycle detection, and load executor. Real failure is orchestration of graph analysis with execution suppression for both plugins. Rejection gate: System was considered but disqualified because behavior text is graph semantics and loader control flow, not OS/network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B where A depends on B and B depends on A; each entry module/onLoad records execution if reached. Run loader and assert: (1) cycle error is reported with both plugin names, (2) neither plugin entry module side effect occurred, and (3) neither onLoad was invoked.

behaviors.description: A plugin's onLoad hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: Seam map: module/interface seam between module importer and lifecycle hook invoker. The defect is sequencing across these components; a unit test of hook runner or importer alone cannot prove ordering. Rejection gate: higher System level is disqualified because behavior requires runtime sequencing inside one Node process, not external subprocess/OS/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Build a plugin whose entry module sets a global/module-level flag during import, and whose onLoad asserts that flag is already set (and records pass/fail). Run loader and assert onLoad runs and sees the flag set, proving import+execution happened first.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its onLoad hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: Seam map: module/interface seam across hook execution, error handling, and dependency-aware scheduler. Real failure occurs in interaction: exception from one plugin must not abort unrelated plugins, while dependent plugins should be skipped/failed appropriately. Integration brake check: no required real OS/network/subprocess; no model-judgment dependency; no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Create three plugins: F (throws in onLoad), U (unrelated), and D (depends on F). Run loader and assert: (1) F failure is reported, (2) U onLoad still executes successfully, (3) D is not initialized because dependency failed, and (4) process continues without global abort.
